steps.text: To find the slope of a line of best fit, identify two points the line clearly passes through. From the scatterplot the line passes through approximately \((1, 3)\) and \((10, 9)\).
Apply the slope formula: \(m = \frac{y_2 - y_1}{x_2 - x_1} = \frac{9 - 3}{10 - 1} = \frac{6}{9} \approx 0.67\).

• A: 0.60 — closest to 0.67 among the choices. That matches choice A — A is correct.
  • B: 2.50 — would mean the line rises 2.5 units for every 1 unit right, far steeper than the scatterplot shows. Eliminated.
  • C: 7.80 — nearly vertical; inconsistent with the gradual upward trend shown. Eliminated.
  • D: 8.00 — similarly extreme; eliminated.
